Title: Pro-oxidant activity of indicaxanthin from Opuntia ficus indica modulates arachidonate metabolism and prostaglandin synthesis through lipid peroxide production in LPS-stimulated RAW 264.7 macrophages

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Common upstream prostaglandin branch forming PGH2
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts arachidonic acid to prostaglandin H2 via cyclooxygenase and peroxidase activities
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strongly induced by LPS; further up-regulated by indicaxanthin in LPS-stimulated RAW 264.7 macrophages
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Indicaxanthin further increases COX-2 expression in LPS-stimulated macrophages and supports flux toward PGD2 and downstream 15-deoxy-PGJ2 production
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LPS induced COX-2 mRNA and protein in RAW 264.7 macrophages. 2. Indicaxanthin pre-treatment further up-regulated COX-2 up to about threefold over LPS alone. 3. Increased COX-2 was associated with reduced PGE2 and increased PGD2/15-deoxy-PGJ2 output when downstream synthase balance was shifted.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; Western blot; prostaglandin metabolite quantification
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key upstream control point for prostaglandin biosynthesis, but not explicitly established as rate-limiting in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Up-regulation of COX-2 is generally associated with inflammatory eicosanoid production, but here flux is redirected toward anti-inflammatory PGD2/15-deoxy-PGJ2 under indicaxanthin treatment
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: LPS stimulation; indicaxanthin pre-treatment; DPI; α-tocopherol
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Limited direct crop breeding relevance; indicates that modulation of upstream eicosanoid enzymes can redirect inflammatory lipid mediator output
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: 15-deoxy-Δ12,14-PGJ2 is anti-inflammatory and linked to resolution of inflammation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study is in murine macrophages and concerns phytochemical modulation rather than plant gene engineering.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Isomerizes prostaglandin H2 to prostaglandin E2
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strongly induced by LPS; down-regulated by indicaxanthin in LPS-stimulated RAW 264.7 macrophages
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Indicaxanthin suppresses mPGES-1 expression and lowers pro-inflammatory PGE2 production in LPS-stimulated macrophages
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LPS markedly induced mPGES-1 mRNA and protein. 2. Indicaxanthin pre-treatment down-regulated mPGES-1 in a concentration-dependent manner. 3. Reduced mPGES-1 expression correlated with decreased PGE2 accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key branch-selecting step for PGE2 formation
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Lowering mPGES-1 may suppress pro-inflammatory signaling but could alter normal prostaglandin homeostasis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Main value is biomedical/metabolic regulation insight rather than plant engineering
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: PGE2 is a pro-inflammatory prostaglandin mediator
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper links mPGES-1 down-regulation mechanistically to inhibition of NF-κB activation by indicaxanthin.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: PGD2 branch leading to cyclopentenone prostaglandins
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Isomerizes prostaglandin H2 to prostaglandin D2
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Modestly induced by LPS in macrophages
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Up-regulated by indicaxanthin in LPS-stimulated RAW 264.7 macrophages
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Indicaxanthin increases H-PGDS expression, enhancing PGD2 production and downstream anti-inflammatory 15-deoxy-PGJ2 formation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LPS alone caused only modest elevation of H-PGDS. 2. Indicaxanthin significantly up-regulated H-PGDS mRNA and protein in a concentration-dependent manner. 3. H-PGDS induction correlated with PGD2 accumulation and subsequent 15-deoxy-PGJ2 production.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; Western blot; PGD2 and 15-deoxy-PGJ2 quantification; pharmacological inhibition context with DPI and α-tocopherol
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Branch-switch enzyme directing PGH2 toward PGD2/15-deoxy-PGJ2 axis
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: LPS stimulation; indicaxanthin pre-treatment; DPI; α-tocopherol
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Primarily relevant as a metabolic branch target for redox-dependent control of anti-inflammatory lipid mediators
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: PGD2 and 15-deoxy-Δ12,14-PGJ2 are associated with anti-inflammatory/resolution phases
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: 15-deoxy-PGJ2 is formed spontaneously downstream of PGD2 rather than directly by H-PGDS.

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation; indicaxanthin-mediated inhibition of activation under LPS
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent controller of prostaglandin branch remodeling through control of inflammatory gene expression, especially mPGES-1 and likely COX-2
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: LPS activates NF-κB; indicaxanthin inhibits NF-κB activation, consistent with reduced mPGES-1 expression
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Prostaglandin biosynthesis branch regulation during inflammatory activation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Indicaxanthin inhibits NF-κB activation in LPS-stimulated macrophages, associated with reduced mPGES-1 expression and decreased PGE2 production
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LPS increased NF-κB reporter activity about fourfold in RAW 264.7 macrophages. 2. Indicaxanthin pre-treatment inhibited NF-κB activation in a concentration-dependent manner. 3. The inhibition was proposed to account for mPGES-1 down-regulation and the shift away from PGE2 production.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: NF-κB luciferase reporter assay; correlation with qRT-PCR, Western blot, and prostaglandin profiling
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Reporter activation increased by LPS and suppressed by indicaxanthin
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Broad NF-κB inhibition may affect multiple inflammatory and immune response programs beyond prostaglandin metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful conceptual regulator for controlling inflammatory lipid metabolism, though outside plant breeding context
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: PGE2 promotes inflammation, whereas PGD2/15-deoxy-Δ12,14-PGJ2 support anti-inflammatory resolution
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: NF-κB is a host regulatory factor measured as pathway activity, not a plant gene from Opuntia.
